Off Calendar Feb-21-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF KELLY MCCLEARY's Motion For Reconsideration Of February 6, 2025 Order Granting Defendant'S Moton To Compel Discovery Responses And Imposing Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The matter is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with CCP 1005, which requires all moving and supporting papers to be served and filed at least 16 court days before the hearing.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant?S Supplemental Form Interrogatories ? General, Set One, Supplemental Form Interrogatories ? Employment, Set One, Supplemental Requests For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two, Special Interrogatories, Set One, Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three
Off Calendar Feb-06-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT PACIFIC NEUROPHYSIOLOGICAL SYSTEMS LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant's Supplemental Form Interrogatories General, Set One, Supplemental Form Interrogatories Employment, Set One, Supplemental Requests For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two, Special Interrogatories, Set One, Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Pacific Neurophysiological Systems LLC's motion to compel further responses is granted. The requested items are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence, and as to its requests for production, Defendant has stated specific facts showing why further production is warranted. Plaintiff Kelly Ann McCleary is ordered to provide substantive responses in compliance with the Code of Civil Procedure to Defendant's Supplemental Form Interrogatories - General, Set One; Supplemental Form Interrogatories - Employment, Set One; Supplemental Requests for Production of Documents, Sets One and Two; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Requests for Production of Documents, Set Three. Plaintiff is ordered to provide these responses within 20 days of notice of entry of this order.

Defendant also seeks sanctions. Sanctions shall be entered except where the party who unsuccessfully opposes a discovery motion acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make the entry of sanctions unjust. Here, the court does not find that Plaintiff acted with substantial justification but notes that Plaintiff's counsel withdrew after the motion was filed and Plaintiff has not apparently obtained new counsel. The court also notes, based on Plaintiff's appearance at the hearing on her prior counsel's motion to withdraw as well as the notice of this discovery motion hearing which was given in the order on that motion, that Plaintiff had notice of this hearing and Defendant's motion. The court awards $250 in sanctions to Defendant, payable via Defendant's counsel. Plaintiff shall pay the sanctions within 20 days of notice of entry of this order.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
